title: Termination and quantitative step-count bound for collection

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
A complete answer to the complexity question must first prove that the collection
algorithm terminates and bound the **number of collection steps** $N_{\text{steps}}$ as a
function of the input length $\ell$, Hirsch length $n$, and exponent magnitudes.

**What must be shown.**
1. Collection (from the left) on any input word of length $\ell$ in $g_1^{\pm1},\dots,g_n^{\pm1}$
   halts with the unique normal form $g_1^{a_1}\cdots g_n^{a_n}$. Consistency of the PCP is the
   hypothesis that makes the result independent of the order of relation application; this must
   be invoked explicitly (cf. Sims, *Computation with FP Groups*, Ch. 9).
2. A **quantitative** bound on $N_{\text{steps}}$ via a well-founded weight/potential
   $\Phi=\sum_i c_i\,(\text{weight of uncollected }g_i)$ whose value strictly decreases at each
   step. The choice of weights $c_i$ and the proof of strict decrease are the content.

**Subtlety to address.** A power relation $g_i^{r_i}=w_{i,i}$ with nontrivial RHS
$w_{i,i}=g_{i+1}^{e_{i+1}}\cdots g_n^{e_n}$ can *re-introduce* generators to the right and
lengthen the uncollected word; the termination measure must dominate this. State whether
$N_{\text{steps}}$ is polynomial in $(\ell,n)$ or whether it inherits the exponent-magnitude
dependence (see the exponent-blow-up issue). This number is the natural unit before charging
arithmetic cost.
